code:
from dataclasses import dataclass

@dataclass
class Triple:
    subject: str
    predicate: str
    object: str

# EVOLVE-BLOCK-START

def predict(triples: list[Triple]) -> str:
    """
    Convert a list of food‑related triples into a single fluent sentence.
    """
    if not triples:
        return ""

    # Build graph of relationships
    graph = {}
    reverse_graph = {}  # object -> (subject, predicate)
    for t in triples:
        s, p, o = t.subject, t.predicate, t.object
        if s not in graph:
            graph[s] = []
        graph[s].append((p, o))
        if o not in reverse_graph:
            reverse_graph[o] = []
        reverse_graph[o].append((s, p))

    # Find the main subject (food item - typically has most food-related predicates)
    food_predicates = {"ingredient", "mainIngredient", "dishVariation", "course",
                       "servingTemperature", "served", "carbohydrate", "fat", "protein",
                       "year", "related", "similarDish", "alternativeName", "creator",
                       "creatorOfDish", "servingSize"}

    main_subject = None
    max_food_preds = 0

    for subject in graph:
        food_count = sum(1 for p, o in graph[subject] if p in food_predicates)
        if food_count > max_food_preds:
            max_food_preds = food_count
            main_subject = subject

    if not main_subject:
        main_subject = list(graph.keys())[0]

    # Build predicate phrase mapping
    phrases = {
        "country": f"from {{obj}}",
        "region": f"originating in {{obj}}",
        "ingredient": f"containing {{obj}}",
        "mainIngredient": f"with main ingredients {{obj}}",
        "dishVariation": f"variations include {{obj}}",
        "course": f"as a {{obj}}",
        "servingTemperature": f"served {{obj}}",
        "served": f"served {{obj}}",
        "carbohydrate": f"with {{obj}} carbohydrates",
        "fat": f"with {{obj}} fat",
        "protein": f"with {{obj}} protein",
        "year": f"introduced in {{obj}}",
        "related": f"related to {{obj}}",
        "similarDish": f"similar to {{obj}}",
        "alternativeName": f"also known as {{obj}}",
        "creator": f"created by {{obj}}",
        "creatorOfDish": f"created by {{obj}}",
        "servingSize": f"with serving size {{obj}}",
        "division": f"division {{obj}}",
        "family": f"family {{obj}}",
        "order": f"order {{obj}}",
        "genus": f"genus {{obj}}",
        "capital": f"capital is {{obj}}",
        "isPartOf": f"part of {{obj}}",
        "foundationPlace": f"found in {{obj}}",
        "leader": f"led by {{obj}}",
        "language": f"language {{obj}}",
        "officialLanguage": f"official language {{obj}}",
        "currency": f"currency {{obj}}",
        "demonym": f"demonym {{obj}}",
        "ethnicGroup": f"ethnic group {{obj}}",
        "leaderTitle": f"leader title {{obj}}",
        "class": f"class {{obj}}"
    }

    def get_predicate_phrase(p, o):
        template = phrases.get(p, "{{p}} {{obj}}")
        return template.replace("{p}", p).replace("{obj}", o)

    # Build all clauses starting from main subject
    visited = set()
    all_clauses = []

    def process_subject(subject, prefix=""):
        if subject in visited:
            return
        visited.add(subject)

        if subject in graph:
            clauses = []
            for p, o in graph[subject]:
                if p in ["country", "region", "isPartOf"]:
                    clauses.append(f"which is {get_predicate_phrase(p, o)}")
                else:
                    clauses.append(f"{get_predicate_phrase(p, o)}")

            if clauses:
                all_clauses.append((subject, clauses))

            # Process connected subjects through reverse graph
            if subject in reverse_graph:
                for obj, pred in reverse_graph[subject]:
                    if obj not in visited:
                        process_subject(obj, f"where the {pred} is {subject}")

    process_subject(main_subject)

    # Build final sentence
    if not all_clauses:
        return main_subject + "."

    parts = []
    for subject, clauses in all_clauses:
        if len(clauses) == 1:
            parts.append(f"{subject} {clauses[0]}")
        else:
            all_but_last = ", ".join(clauses[:-1])
            parts.append(f"{subject} {all_but_last}, and {clauses[-1]}")

    if len(parts) == 1:
        sentence = parts[0]
    else:
        sentence = parts[0]
        for part in parts[1:]:
            sentence += f", {part}"

    return sentence + "."

# EVOLVE-BLOCK-END

The program must implement a function called 'predict' that accepts a list of triples and generates a coherent, contextually relevant sentence that accurately represents the information contained in the triples. Ensure that the generated text is fluent, grammatically correct, and maintains the meaning of the original data. There can be multiple triples given that make up a complex sentence. Triples:
(Antwerp | cityServed | Antwerp International Airport)
(Belgium | country | Antwerp)
(City of Brussels | capital | Belgium)
Example sentence:
"Antwerp International Airport serves the city of Antwerp which is in Belgium, where the capital is Brussels."

```python
from dataclasses import dataclass

@dataclass
class Triple:
    subject: str
    predicate: str
    object: str

# EVOLVE-BLOCK-START

def predict(triples: list[Triple]) -> str:
    """
    Convert a list of food‑related triples into a single fluent sentence.
    """
    if not triples:
        return ""

    # Build graph of relationships
    graph = {}
    for t in triples:
        s, p, o = t.subject, t.predicate, t.object
        if s not in graph:
            graph[s] = []
        graph[s].append((p, o))

    # Find the main subject (food item - typically has most food-related predicates)
    food_predicates = {"ingredient", "mainIngredient", "dishVariation", "course",
                       "servingTemperature", "served", "carbohydrate", "fat", "protein",
                       "year", "related", "similarDish", "alternativeName", "creator",
                       "creatorOfDish", "servingSize"}

    main_subject = None
    max_food_preds = 0

    for subject in graph:
        food_count = sum(1 for p, o in graph[subject] if p in food_predicates)
        if food_count > max_food_preds:
            max_food_preds = food_count
            main_subject = subject

    if not main_subject:
        main_subject = list(graph.keys())[0]

    # Build connected sentence chain
    visited = set()
    parts = []

    def get_predicate_phrase(p, o):
        phrases = {
            "country": f"from {o}",
            "region": f"originating in {o}",
            "ingredient": f"containing {o}",
            "mainIngredient": f"with main ingredients {o}",
            "dishVariation": f"variations include {o}",
            "course": f"as a {o}",
            "servingTemperature": f"served {o}",
            "served": f"served {o}",
            "carbohydrate": f"with {o} carbohydrates",
            "fat": f"with {o} fat",
            "protein": f"with {o} protein",
            "year": f"introduced in {o}",
            "related": f"related to {o}",
            "similarDish": f"similar to {o}",
            "alternativeName": f"also known as {o}",
            "creator": f"created by {o}",
            "creatorOfDish": f"created by {o}",
            "servingSize": f"with serving size {o}",
            "division": f"division {o}",
            "family": f"family {o}",
            "order": f"order {o}",
            "genus": f"genus {o}",
            "capital": f"capital {o}",
            "isPartOf": f"part of {o}",
            "foundationPlace": f"found in {o}",
            "leader": f"led by {o}",
            "language": f"language {o}",
            "officialLanguage": f"official language {o}",
            "currency": f"currency {o}",
            "demonym": f"demonym {o}",
            "ethnicGroup": f"ethnic group {o}",
            "leaderTitle": f"leader title {o}",
            "class": f"class {o}"
        }
        return phrases.get(p, f"{p} {o}")

    # Process main subject and its direct relationships
    if main_subject in graph:
        clauses = []
        for p, o in graph[main_subject]:
            if p in ["country", "region", "isPartOf"]:
                clauses.append(f"which is {get_predicate_phrase(p, o)}")
            else:
                clauses.append(f"{get_predicate_phrase(p, o)}")

        if clauses:
            if len(clauses) == 1:
                parts.append(f"{main_subject} {clauses[0]}")
            else:
                all_but_last = ", ".join(clauses[:-1])
                parts.append(f"{main_subject} {all_but_last}, and {clauses[-1]}")

    # Handle connected subjects (like country -> capital)
    for subject, relations in graph.items():
        if subject == main_subject or subject in visited:
            continue

        for p, o in relations:
            if subject in parts[0] if parts else False:
                continue
            if p in ["capital", "leader", "currency", "language", "officialLanguage", "demonym", "ethnicGroup"]:
                for prev_part in parts:
                    if o in prev_part:
                        parts.append(f"where the {p} is {subject}")
                        break

    if not parts:
        return main_subject + "."

    sentence = parts[0]
    for i, part in enumerate(parts[1:], 1):
        if i == 1:
            sentence += f", and {part}"
        else:
            sentence += f", {part}"

    return sentence + "."

# EVOLVE-BLOCK-END
